items.title: Ngambri people say ACT government has not upheld recognition agreement

children.content: The ACT government has been accused of failing to uphold its agreement with the Ngambri people over the recognition of traditional ownership of the Canberra area.

gallery.altText: A greying Aboriginal man crouches beside an older Aboriginal woman standing beside a tree that has been carved with a design

thumbnail.altText: A greying Aboriginal man crouches beside an older Aboriginal woman standing beside a tree that has been carved with a design

items.title: Canberra man faces trial for attempting to murder his wife after night out

children.content: A jury hears a Canberra man on trial for allegedly attempting to murder his wife whispered "I'm going to kill you" in her ear shortly before stabbing her in the arm with a kitchen knife.

image.altText: The exterior of a concrete building that reads "Law Courts of the Australian Capital Territory" over the entrance.

items.title: Ben Roberts-Smith's bail conditions varied to attend war memorial event

children.content: War veteran Ben Roberts-Smith's bail conditions have been varied so he can attend an opening at the Australian War Memorial in Canberra next week.

image.altText: Ben Roberts-Smith exiting Downing Centre Sydney, smiling, in a navy suit.

items.title: Brother believes pregnant 20yo Canberra woman could have been Ivan Milat's first victim

children.content: A family says its private investigation suggests Keren Rowland's 1971 disappearance and death is linked to serial killer Ivan Milat as an inquiry hears claims of missing records, lost exhibits and unanswered questions.

gallery.altText: A shotgun-toting man with a handlebar moustache and broad-brimmed hat poses for a photo.
An old, grainy photo of a young woman.
Man sitting in a chair in front of microphone.

thumbnail.altText: A shotgun-toting man with a handlebar moustache and broad-brimmed hat poses for a photo.
An old, grainy photo of a young woman.
Man sitting in a chair in front of microphone.

items.title: Former ACT politician faces new charges after arrest for child grooming

children.content: Former ACT attorney-general Gordon Ramsay was to have been committed to trial in the ACT Supreme Court today, but instead his lawyers have asked for more time to prepare the case as he is facing seven additional charges.

image.altText: A man with short grey hair wearing a dark blue suit and glasses walks outside a court building looking serious.

items.title: Paramedic 'haunted' by woman's death at hands of partner has since quit job

children.content: Those affected by the murder of Tiffany Spence, who died from horrific injuries inflicted by her partner Adam Britt, tell a sentencing hearing of the long-term impacts of her death.

image.altText: A woman wearing a purple singlet top.

items.title: Drug harm advocates say recriminalising meth ignores evidence, adds stigma

children.content: Drug harm reduction advocates says there is little evidence that methamphetamine is more harmful in the ACT since being decriminalised, despite a Canberra Liberals commitment to recriminalise it if elected.

gallery.altText: Three large white crystals on a brown tray with a piece of foil nearby.

thumbnail.altText: Three large white crystals on a brown tray with a piece of foil nearby.

items.title: Trump comes face to face with the targets of his ire at G7

children.content: In France, the US president finds himself among a group of emboldened counterparts, many of whom now seem more comfortable criticising him publicly than ever before.

articles.title: A close look at Iran's 14-point plan reveals what Tehran could gain

children.content: Iran agreeing to a ceasefire deal with the United States tells us Tehran likely had something to gain from signing on. This is what Iran is promoting as the 14-point deal.

image.altText: An image of Donald Trump in front of a map of Iran.

articles.title: Trump intensifies pressure on Netanyahu over Lebanon attacks

children.content: The US president levels more criticism at Israel's prime minister and suggests asking Syria to take on Hezbollah "if Israel can't do the job without killing everyone else".

image.altText: Donald Trump stands in front of a 'G7 France' sign.

articles.title: Farmer fertiliser hope as Iran ceasefire promises to reopen supply

children.content: Farmers are hoping the US-Iran deal will help free up fertiliser supply and lower costs during the winter cropping season.

image.altText: A hand holds brilliant white fertiliser while a truck tips it off in the background

gallery.altText: Two men in suits, speaking and pointing.

thumbnail.altText: Two men in suits, speaking and pointing.

avatar.alt: A woman smiling at the camera.

items.title: Glue Store closes after posting $8.4m loss as economic pain hits retailers

children.content: Glue Store announces its permanent closure, becoming the latest Australian retailer to fail this year.

image.altText: A closed sign on a Melbourne business.

items.title: ASX up, oil plunges to three-month low while SpaceX becomes fifth-biggest US company — as it happened

children.content: Oil prices sink to a three-month low on reports the US will allow Iran to start selling oil again, while the Australian share market edges up after a sell-off across Wall Street's tech and AI stocks.

image.altText: The ASX building exterior.

images.altText: Lionel Messi holds his hands out wide

articles.title: Quick hits: Mbappé's stunning goal and why this coach refused to pose

children.content: Kylian Mbappé's magical strike thrust him back into the World Cup spotlight, while master coach Marcelo Bielsa did everything he could to avoid it. French journalists leave an empty space and Jesus gets a Mexican kit makeover. Here's the five quick hits from the FIFA World Cup.

image.altText: Kylian Mbappé, Roberto Lopes and Marcelo Bielsa composite.

articles.title: Iraqi scores goal in World Cup debut after being detained by US customs

children.content: Iraq striker Aymen Hussein was delayed on the way to his first World Cup, but he still made the best of his tournament debut.

image.altText: Aymen Hussein cleebrates a goal for Iraq at 2026 World Cup.

articles.title: Socceroo's blunt reply to USA's claim at World Cup

children.content: Socceroos defender Alessandro Circati offers a blunt reply to Sebastian Berhalter's comments ahead of the World Cup clash between the two sides.

image.altText: Alessandro Circati celebrates after Socceroos' win over Türkiye at FIFA World Cup.

children.content: Lionel Messi scores three as Argentina beats Algeria, after Erling Haaland scored twice as Norway beat Iraq 4-1 in Boston and Kylian Mbappé's super strike sunk Senegal. Follow live.

items.title: Messi equals World Cup record with hat-trick, as Austria wins over Jordan

updates.title: FT: Austria gets the three points after a thrilling contest against Jordan

items.title: Cambodia says 310,000 suspected scammers left country amid crackdown

children.content: A report by Amnesty International casts doubt on the impact of Cambodia's high-profile scam industry crackdown, finding authorities failed to dismantle the "vast majority" of operations.

image.altText: A drone shot of a prison-like compound with a series of long buildings with blue roofs surrounding a square centre building.

items.title: AFL stars' mother recounts visit from Dezi Freeman detectives

children.content: Kay Reid, a regional Victorian businesswoman and the mother of footballers Ben and Sam Reid, confirms police searched her home as part of Taskforce Summit's investigation into the movements of police killer Dezi Freeman.

image.altText: A police officer stands near two others who sit at a table with a bald man in a green shirt who look directly at the camera.

items.title: Zoo raises chimp on human colostrum after mum accidentally drops newborn

children.content: Rockhampton Zoo is hand-rearing a chimp whose mother dropped it from a height by accident hours after giving birth.

image.altText: A baby chimp drinking milk from a bottle.

gallery.altText: A human cradles a baby chimpanzee with black fur as it drinks from a bottle of milk.

thumbnail.altText: A human cradles a baby chimpanzee with black fur as it drinks from a bottle of milk.

avatar.alt: A woman in a black shirt with long hair smiling at the camera

items.title: Two high-profile Star casino executives banned from running companies for years

children.content: Two former executives of The Star Entertainment Group Ltd have been handed penalties for breaching the Corporations Act in connection with a Chinese money-laundering scandal.

image.altText: Matt Bekier addresses a crowd

items.title: Gina Rinehart's iron ore company flags job losses, reportedly in the hundreds

children.content: Gina Rinehart's Hancock Iron Ore has cut jobs a year after merging its two iron ore businesses into a single entity, but won't say how many.

image.altText: Gina Rinehart stands at a podium.

items.title: What happened when Ann Patchett quit swearing for good

children.content: The bestselling author of books including Bel Canto, Tom Lake and her latest, Whistler, is determined to see the good in everyone.

gallery.altText: Ann Patchett, a woman with short greying hair, wearing a floral dress, sitting in front a series of bookshelves.

thumbnail.altText: Ann Patchett, a woman with short greying hair, wearing a floral dress, sitting in front a series of bookshelves.

items.title: Six gaming experts share their favourite video games of the year so far

children.content: From cosy builders to hectic shooters and all the way through to nostalgic adventures, there are lots of gems to enjoy on your favourite game console so far this year.

image.altText: Three people shaped characters stand with a pokemon trubbish

items.title: Bluey speaks Indigenous language in time for NAIDOC week

children.content: The animated award-winning cartoon series Bluey has been translated into Yolŋu Matha, from north-east Arnhem Land, for NAIDOC Week.

image.altText: Bluey is sitting at the beach playing with a shell.

items.title: Two men charged over alleged kidnapping murder of Sydney grandfather

children.content: Robbery and Serious Crime Squad detectives have charged another two men following an investigation into the alleged kidnapping and murder of Chris Baghsarian earlier this year in Sydney's north.

gallery.altText: A young man with his head in his hands sitting on the ground, three officers standing either side of him.

thumbnail.altText: A young man with his head in his hands sitting on the ground, three officers standing either side of him.

items.title: Sandilands reaches settlement over $100m radio contract termination

children.content: Veteran Sydney broadcaster Kyle Sandilands and ARN Media reach a settlement to end a $100 million contract dispute, which had been playing out in the Federal Court.

image.altText: Sandilands wearing a navy suit and sunglasses

items.title: 'Deeply ashamed': Paedophile's handwritten message to victims

children.content: Investigators were knocking on the door of a disgraced Sydney childcare worker within a week of him posting images of children on the darknet after identifying the user through metadata and CCTV.

image.altText: A CCTV still of David James buying a drink at the Orchard Hotel.

items.title: What a trillion dollars costs democracy

children.content: Democracy cannot survive trillionaires. We need to start taxing billionaires properly.

gallery.altText: Elon Musk wearing a suit behind a dark backdrop.

thumbnail.altText: Elon Musk wearing a suit behind a dark backdrop.

items.title: Six years after daughter's death, mother still waiting for mental health reforms

children.content: The mother of Kate Savage, whose death at 13 sparked a review of mental health supports for children, says she's lost faith promises of reform will ever be kept.

image.altText: A young girl poses for a close up photo

items.title: Runaway Tasmanian devil Mary recaptured after two-week search

children.content: A spokesperson for Village Roadshow Theme Parks said Mary, the two-year-old marsupial, was found and recaptured overnight.

image.altText: Tasmanian devil Mary CCTV

items.title: Pastoralists take to the skies to track livestock lost in floods

children.content: Closed roads and flooded plains are making livestock inaccessible for some pastoralists, while others are employing some serious technology to track down their animals.

image.altText: A small sheep up to its knees in dirty water and mud

items.title: The night Scotland painted Boston's baseball heartland in tartan

children.content: Throughout the famed but understated bleachers of Boston's Fenway Park, an army of Scottish football fans created a night few fans will ever forget.

items.title: 'I didn't have a life': Pensioner moves to Thailand for cheaper living

children.content: Peter Stevenson says moving to Thailand has allowed him to live more comfortably on his Australian Age Pension after struggling to cover rent and essentials in regional Victoria.

image.altText: A selfie of a smiling man in silver-rimmed glasses and a grey tee taken from inside a restauraunt with red lanterns.

items.title: Australia softens Middle East travel warnings after months of disruption

children.content: Australia has downgraded its Middle East travel advice for key Gulf hubs, easing warnings from "Do Not Travel" to "Reconsider your need to travel".

image.altText: Sign at airport

items.title: MP joins conservative Family First party ahead of abortion bill debate

children.content: SA upper house MP Sarah Game has abandoned the political party she founded to join the conservative Family First brand, marking the third time she has changed parties in less than two years.

image.altText: A woman speaks to microphones while a man looks on

items.title: Marauding cockatoos thwarted by bin lid invented by men's shed

children.content: Residents of the scenic Great Ocean Road are at their wits' end after the increasingly intelligent birds learnt how to lift the lids of wheelie bins, spreading rubbish through the town of Lorne.

image.altText: A white cockatoo sits on top of a wooden table beside cutlery, sauce and a sign that says "cocktails".

items.title: Prince George heading to Eton College to continue education

children.content: Prince George, who turns 13 in July, will attend Eton College this September, following in the footsteps of his father, Prince William.

items.title: New SA laws would remove serious offenders' rights over victims' remains

children.content: The South Australian government will introduce legislation to remove the rights of perpetrators of serious crimes to decide what happens with the remains of their victims.

image.altText: A woman with long brown hair wearing a black top holds a photograph of a young man and woman

items.title: Gout runs a stunning third as Lyles breaks world record

children.content: Rising Australian superstar Gout Gout pushes American champion Noah Lyles all the way in their first race against each other.

image.altText: Two men hug after a running race.

items.title: Men tear gassed in Darwin's youth prison have payout slashed by High Court

children.content: The High Court slashes an award for exemplary damages for four former inmates from Darwin's notorious Don Dale Youth Detention Centre who were affected by a tear gas incident in 2014.

image.altText: The exterior of the Don Dale Youth Detention Centre, with barbed wire in the foreground.
